summary: Stopped before reading source content or mutating files because the delivered repository context failed its required byte-count and SHA-256 bindings.

artifacts.name: release-review-lifecycle-correction-r10.md
artifacts.content:
# Release Review Lifecycle Correction R10

## Outcome

Blocked during mandatory context verification. No implementation, Git-state change, or file mutation occurred.

## Verification Evidence

Thirteen declared context files were unavailable at their exact repository locators: ADR-0005, Candidate 3 retirement evidence, all four release-correction-r9 artifacts, the release-gate script and test, both R2 review scripts, and all three release-correction-r10 input documents.

`package.json` was present but failed both bindings. Expected 2,946 bytes and SHA-256 `43414ace7e294a9a173ee78ab33baa5791959921f5da2a1c7b3b69d081938df2`; received 2,549 bytes and SHA-256 `52acc84e561245f27afe26af16c000ce5d1e121b5649c4e70c2d03ec140c7be7`.

The contract requires an immediate stop when any declared source is missing or mismatched, so no source content was used and no focused verification commands were run.

risks: The active worktree appears not to contain the exact approved candidate context; proceeding would violate the source-binding and candidate-identity requirements.
followUps: Re-deliver or activate the exact approved candidate worktree containing every declared source at its bound byte count and SHA-256 digest, then rerun this specialist contract.
